title: Foundations of Accounting
description: Introduction to financial and managerial accounting theory and practice with emphasis on the role of accounting information in business decisions.

title: Financial Reporting and Analysis
description: Theory and practice that underlies the preparation, analysis, and use of financial statements.

title: Federal Income Taxation
description: Fundamentals and concepts of federal income taxation. Emphasis on tax strategy, business taxation, and individual taxation. Topics include tax strategy modeling, gross income, deductions, credits, tax determination, property transactions, business entities, and basics of international taxation.

title: Honors: Foundations of Accounting
description: Introduction to financial accounting theory and practice with emphasis on the role of financial information in business decisions. The course will make extensive use of computer technology for retrieving and analyzing financial information.

title: Cost Management
description: Cost information for products, services, and how cost information is recorded, analyzed, reported, and used in decision making. Topics include cost concepts and behavior, cost systems, budgeting, activity-based costing and management, and strategic cost management.

title: Advanced Financial Reporting
description: Accounting standards for advanced financial reporting topics, such as statement of cash flows, income taxes, leases, accounting changes, consolidated financial statements, and foreign operations.

title: Accounting Internship
description: Grading Restriction: Satisfactory/No Credit grading only.

title: Advertising and Public Relations Design
description: Study, use, and application of design, color, type, and layout styles as they affect concept development in the creation of promotional materials. Analysis of organizational goals and how they mold strategic and conceptual development. Application of relevant computer software for creation of promotional material.

title: Advertising Creative Strategy
description: Basic concepts of creative strategy with intensive practice in developing creative strategy statement, writing and designing advertisements, and judging creative work.

title: Advertising and Public Relations Research Methods
description: Secondary data and primary research techniques for advertising and public relations decisions.

title: Financial Reporting
description: Theory and practice for preparing and using financial statements. Introduction to professionalism and ethics in accounting.

title: Advertising Professional Seminar
description: Exploration of career choices in mass communications. Resume and letter writing, interviewing, and portfolio preparation.

title: Advertising Management
description: Case-study approach to advertising decisions. Data analysis and interpretation, generating alternative strategies, oral and written presentation of recommendations.

title: Advertising Campaigns
description: Group-based development, execution, and evaluation of an advertising campaign for a regional or national client.

title: Advertising Media Strategy
description: Assessment of markets, vehicle audiences, and mathematical techniques for advertising planning. Instruction in media planning, buying, and evaluation.

title: Advertising Issues
description: Examination of the role of advertising in society and controversies surrounding economic, social, cultural, and ethical aspects of advertising. Emphasis on written and oral exposition of different viewpoints.

title: Financial Compliance and Operational Auditing
description: Auditing's role in society from an internal and external perspective, audit methodology, role of internal control and statistical sampling in auditing, fraud auditing, operational auditing, compliance auditing, and application of auditing procedures to specific transaction cycles.

title: Fluid Mechanics I
description: Introduction to fluid flow concepts; hydrostatics; development of mass, momentum, and energy conservation laws in integral and differential form; dimensional analysis and similitude; viscous laminar and turbulent flows in pipes; introduction to boundary layers.

title: Aerospace Seminar
description: An overview of aerospace engineering with lectures, laboratory demonstrations, and field trips. Aerospace history, aircraft and space flight fundamentals, propulsion techniques, wind tunnel testing, biomedical issues in aviation and space flight.

title: Independent Study
description: Individual study in a specialized area under the supervision of a faculty member.

title: Aerospace Engineering Instrumentation and Measurement
description: Fundamentals of measurement systems; standards; dynamic characteristics of instruments; statistical data treatment; transducers; signal conditioning; strain, pressure, temperature and flow measurements.

title: Special Topics
description: Detailed study of a specialized area of advertising. Topics vary by semester and include advanced media strategy, advanced creative strategy, direct marketing, and multicultural advertising.

title: Structural Analysis of Aerospace Vehicles
description: Fundamentals of structural analysis applied to configurations common to aerospace vehicles.

title: Aerodynamics
description: Theory and design of aerodynamic bodies for desired characteristics. Potential flow theory, viscous effects, and compressibility effects. Subsonic, transonic, and supersonic airfoils.

title: Professional Topics
description: Topics relating to professional responsibility, communications, and organization. Formal oral presentation by each student on an engineering topic chosen by the student and approved by the instructor.

title: Compressible Flow
description: One-dimensional internal flow with shocks, friction, and nonadiabatic conditions. Two-dimensional external flows.

title: Astronautics
description: Solar system; orbital mechanics; propulsion; atmospheric entry, including thermal protection materials, human factors in space flight, the space environment, and current topics.

title: Introduction to Aerospace Design
description: Design process, synthesis, design studies. Individual design reports required.

title: Propulsion
description: Principles of propulsion devices, principally jet and rocket engines. Combustion and equilibrium, inlets, nozzles, compressors, pumps and turbines. Overall performance evaluations.

title: Aerospace System Design
description: Synthesis and design of a complete aerospace system. Participation in team design effort including formal presentations and design report.

title: Aerospace Engineering Laboratory
description: Designing, conducting, and reporting results of experimental exercises. Test standards and specifications. Analysis of data and formation of conclusions.

title: Selected Topics in Aerospace Engineering
description: Problems and topics related to developments and practice in aerospace engineering.

title: Art of Africa, Oceania, and Pre-Columbian America
description: (See Art History 162.)

description: Multidisciplinary approach to the African-American experience from the Civil War through the Civil Rights era which focuses on such topics as African-American rural and urban societies, the African-American church and education and African-American intellectual and protest movements.

title: Introduction to African Studies
description: Multidisciplinary approach to the study of African traditions, cultures, religions, political economies, pre-colonial democracies, and states from the first through the 16th century. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Introduction to African-American Studies
description: Multidisciplinary approach to the African-American experience through the Civil War period which examines such issues as traditional African societies, the institution of slavery, the development of African-American culture, the beginnings of African-American protest tradition, and the Civil War and Reconstruction.

title: Introduction to African Studies
description: Multidisciplinary study of Africa and its incorporation into the world economy between the 16th and the 20th century. Includes the rise of nationalism, post-colonial dependency, contemporary problems, and current liberation struggles in various areas of the continent. Writing-emphasis course.

title: The African Diaspora
description: (See Anthropology 315.)

title: Caribbean Cultures and Societies
description: (See Anthropology 319.)

title: Introduction to African-American Music
description: (See Musicology 310.)

title: African Literature
description: Survey of the major works and issues in African literature from the mid ― 1960's to the present. Special emphasis placed on the refashioning of the English language to carry an identifiably African experience; focus on fiction and drama, applicable theory and critical terms. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Comparative Studies in African and African-American Societies
description: Comparative studies of African and African-American societies in such areas as education, religion, and social stratification. Includes the respective views African-Americans and Africans have of each other and the concept of Pan-Africanism. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Advertising Principles
description: Survey of the role of advertising in American business and society. Relationship between advertising and marketing; functional components of the advertising process ― research, media, creative, and management.

title: Senior Research Project
description: Students design and implement a research project of their choice in the field of Africana studies. Writing-emphasis course.

title: History and Philosophy of African-American Education
description: Focuses on attempts by African-Americans to secure an education for themselves and their children from the era of slavery to the Brown decision in 1954. Examines black perceptions of the importance of education and special obstacles confronting African-Americans who seek education on the primary, secondary, college, or graduate level. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Research Seminar in Africana Studies
description: Teaches basic approaches to the research process and the development of research skills.

title: Black African Politics
description: (See Political Science 452.)

title: Advertising Practicum
description: Experience in a functional area of advertising.

title: Art and Archaeology of Ancient Africa
description: (See Art History 462.)

title: African-American Art
description: (See Art History 470.)

title: African-American Communities in Urban America
description: Evaluates the benevolent and historical influence of three major institutions, the church, the family, and the school, upon the African-American struggle to survive. Includes political, economic, and social factors utilized by black people in developing coping strategies and mechanisms. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Black Male in American Society
description: Examines historical images, myths, and stereotypes which have developed concerning African-American males in American society. Includes the impact of such critical factors as black feminism, violence, concepts of masculinity, the family, white males, white females, homosexuality, nationalism, and athletics on African-American males in America.

title: Airplane Performance
description: Airplane aerodynamics, characteristics of propulsion systems, prediction of airplane performance, static and dynamic stability, and control of aircraft.

title: Foreign Study
description: Repeatability: May be repeated. Maximum 6 hours.

title: Independent Study
description: Repeatability: May be repeated. Maximum 6 hours.

title: Field Experience in Agricultural and Extension Education
description: Field observation/experience in potential agricultural and extension education career fields. Requirements include daily journal, formal written report, completion of required hours, and seminar.

title: Selected Topics in Aerospace Engineering
description: Problems and topics related to developments and practice in aerospace engineering.

title: Biology of Human Variability
description: (See Anthropology 496.)

title: Off-Campus Study
description: Repeatability: May be repeated. Maximum 6 hours.

title: Non-Formal Youth Development Programs
description: Structured experience in administrating, organizing, conducting, and evaluating youth education programs in agricultural and extension education.

title: Methods of Teaching Agriscience
description: Methods and techniques for teaching agriculture, preparing lesson plans and units of instruction, developing activities for agriculture programs, and utilizing resources, multimedia, and computer technology into instruction. To be taken in the fall prior to student teaching. Includes a lab component.

title: Student Teaching in Agricultural and Extension Education
description: Full-time teaching practicum in an approved high school program. Applied practices needed by agricultural education teachers.

title: Program Planning in Agriscience Education
description: Overview of the historical and philosophical aspect of agriculture education, the role of teacher and learner. Emphasis on SAE, FFA, community service, and summer programs. Includes a lab component.

title: Student Teaching in Agricultural and Extension Education
description: Full-time teaching practicum in an approved high school program. Applied practices needed by agricultural education teachers.

title: Communication Techniques in Agriculture
description: Elements of effective use of mass media in agricultural and extension education. Effective technical writing and presentation strategies for agricultural audiences.

title: Agricultural Leadership Development
description: Identification of styles and roles of leadership; development of leadership techniques and skills required in working with organizations and youth groups; methods of resolving conflict, of communicating, of guiding and evaluating; and ethical considerations for leaders.

title: Internship In Agricultural and Extension Education
description: Pre-approved off-campus supervised experience in county extension offices, agricultural businesses, or agricultural related agencies. Requires living off-campus for a specified time.

title: Independent Study
description: Individualized study of a special project or problem in agricultural and extension education. Must be selected in consultation with the instructor.

title: Leadership Development in Small Groups and Teams
description: Learning about leadership dynamics in small groups and how to be more influential in social and work settings related to the area of agricultural and natural resources.

title: Classic Figures in Leadership
description: Examination of leadership from an applied context. Leadership is analyzed through a variety of genres: autobiography, drama, fiction, tracts and treatises, and speeches.

title: Leadership and Diversity in Organizations and Communities
description: Analysis of the dynamic interactions of personal characteristics, technical skills, interpersonal influence, commitment, goals, and power necessary for both leader and follower effectiveness in complex agricultural and natural resource organizations. Examination of leadership theories and their applications in diverse organizations and communities.

title: Foundations of Agricultural and Extension Education
description: History and philosophy of agricultural education and extension education. Major areas of emphasis include the historical development of agricultural education in the public schools and the federal extension education system. Formal and non-formal methods of education used, audiences served, organizational structure, and programming emphases will be studied by students. Foundation course for departmental majors and service course for those interested in related careers.

title: Introduction to Agricultural and Natural Resource Leadership
description: Analyze and evaluate students’ current beliefs about leadership and recognize and identify behaviors used by effective leaders in the field of agricultural and natural resources.

title: Leadership, Motivation, Power and Influence
description: Classical and contemporary motivation theories as applied to leadership in agricultural and natural resource organizations and communities. Organizational influence processes, power and influence in organizations and communities.

title: Economics of the Global Food and Fiber System
description: Introduction to microeconomic and macroeconomic principles and their application to the global food and fiber system. Specific topics include consumer and producer behavior, market equilibrium, monetary and fiscal policy, and international trade.

title: Internship in Agricultural and Natural Resource Leadership
description: Pre-approved supervised experience with agricultural firm or organization in the area of leadership.

title: Seminar in Agricultural and Natural Resource Leadership
description: Analyzing contemporary issues in the field of agricultural and natural resource leadership.

title: Opportunities in Agricultural Economics and Business
description: Overview of current issues and career opportunities for majors and non-majors.

title: Farm Business Management I
description: Principles and procedures for determining most profitable business organizations and systems of operation; attention to traditional and nontraditional agricultural enterprises and businesses; nature of managerial processes; business records and their uses; budgeting; acquisition and management of capital, land, labor and machinery; farm business planning.

title: The Food and Agricultural Marketing System
description: Survey of U.S. food and fiber marketing system; marketing functions; industry structure; market channels; marketing options of farmers; basic analysis of marketing problems.

title: Quantitative Methods in Agricultural Economics
description: Quantitative analytical tools used in economics and business. Simple and multiple linear regression techniques applied to economic data. Analysis of cross-section and time series data. Optimization techniques applied to economic and business decisions.

title: Topics in African-American Religion
description: (See Religious Studies 353.)

title: Food and Agricultural Policy
description: Values, goals, and policy process. Economic rationale and effects of policy. Historical development and current characteristics of commodity, credit, food, and trade policy.

title: Economics of Biomass for Renewable Energy
Natural Resource Economics
description: Economic analysis of natural resource use and conservation with emphasis on land, water and other renewable resources. Principles for benefit-cost analyses of natural resource projects and policies. Methods for valuation of non-market impacts associated with natural resource use. Tools for analysis of spatial-referenced data.

title: Applied Policy Analysis for Environmental and Natural Resource Management
description: Application of a policy analysis framework to conflicts and issues associated with natural resource use and related environmental quality impacts. Design of institutional changes to improve economic efficiency and equity, with emphasis on the potential applicability of market-type and incentive-based policy mechanisms.

title: Orientation to Studies in Agriculture and Natural Resources
Agriculture and Natural Resources Honors Topics
Introduction to Teambuilding
description: Review of the basic fundamentals of how to work effectively in teams and groups. Basic leadership principles such as communication, leadership styles, trust, and group dynamics are examined through multiple forms of learning activities with a focus on skill sets which can be applied to group formation in community and business environments.

title: Computer Applications to Problem Solving
description: Use of computer technology to analyze and report problems related to agricultural sciences and natural resources. The use and integration of computer applications such as spreadsheets, databases, presentation graphics, word processing, and other applicable software as needed for problem analysis and reporting.

title: International Experience in Agriculture and Natural Resources
Leadership Development in Agriculture and Natural Resources
description: Enrollment limited to College Ambassadors. Readings on leadership and personal development, communication techniques, and/or personality types.

title: Arts of the African Diaspora
description: (See Art History 463.)

title: Honors Research
description: For students participating in the CASNR Honors Program. Consists of independent work with a faculty member.

title: Honors Thesis
description: For students participating in the CASNR Honors Program. Required during the semester when the thesis is written and the presentation is made.

title: The Air Force Today
description: Survey that focuses on the organizational structure and missions of the Air Force; officership and professionalism; and includes an introduction to communicative skills. A weekly Leadership Laboratory (LLAB) consisting of Air Force customs and courtesies, health and physical fitness, and drill and ceremonies is mandatory.

title: Special Topics in Agriculture and Natural Resources
African-American Women in American Society
description: Focuses on historical and contemporary social, economic and political factors in American society as they relate to the black woman. Writing-emphasis course.

title: The Air Force Today
description: Survey that focuses on the organizational structure and missions of the Air Force; officership and professionalism; and includes an introduction to communicative skills. A weekly Leadership Laboratory (LLAB) consisting of Air Force customs and courtesies, health and physical fitness, and drill and ceremonies is mandatory.

title: Leadership Laboratory
description: Includes a study of Air Force customs and courtesies, drill and ceremonies, and giving military commands; instructing, correcting, and evaluating the preceding skills; studying the environment of an Air Force officer; and learning about opportunities available to commissioned officers.

title: Leadership Laboratory
description: Consists of activities classified as advanced leadership experiences. They involve planning, organizing, staffing, coordinating, directing, and controlling the military activities of the cadet corps; preparation and presentation of briefings and other oral and written communications; and providing interviews, guidance, and information which will increase the understanding, motivation, and performance of other cadets.

title: National Security Forces in Contemporary American Society
description: Examines the need for national security, analyzes the evolution and formulation of the American defense policy, strategy, and joint doctrine; investigates the methods for managing conflict; and overviews regional security, arms control, and terrorism. Special topics of interest focus on the military as a profession, officership, the military justice system, and current issues affecting military professionalism. Within this structure, continued emphasis is given to the refinement of communicative skills. A weekly Leadership Laboratory (LLAB) consisting primarily of advanced leadership experiences in office-type activities is mandatory.

title: Introduction to American Studies
description: Explores dynamics and nature of the culture(s) of the United States through interdisciplinary study and interpretation. Considers both mainstream and minority cultures. Writing-emphasis course.

title: National Security Forces in Contemporary American Society
description: Examines the need for national security, analyzes the evolution and formulation of the American defense policy, strategy, and joint doctrine; investigates the methods for managing conflict; and overviews regional security, arms control, and terrorism. Special topics of interest focus on the military as a profession, officership, the military justice system, and current issues affecting military professionalism. Within this structure, continued emphasis is given to the refinement of communicative skills. A weekly Leadership Laboratory (LLAB) consisting primarily of advanced leadership experiences in office-type activities is mandatory.

title: Off-Campus Study
description: Repeatability: May be repeated. Maximum 9 hours.

title: Introduction to Animal Science
description: Preparation of academic plans and career discussion. Introduction to structure and production principles of the food animal and horse industries. Overview of companion and alternative livestock. Market classes and grades of cattle, poultry and poultry products, lamb and wool, and swine.

title: International Agricultural Trade and Marketing
description: Introduction to real and monetary aspects of international trade effect on agricultural commodity flows; partial equilibrium analysis of international trade in agricultural products; institutional aspects of international marketing of agricultural products.

title: Freedom of Speech
description: (See Communication Studies 469.)

title: Foreign Study
description: Repeatability: May be repeated. Maximum 9 hours.

title: Biotechnology and Management Practices in Animal Production
description: Exposure to current animal agriculture management practices and biotechnology techniques as they affect beef, dairy, horse, poultry, sheep and swine industries. Includes animal behavior, restraint and welfare, computer applications, nutrients and nutrient utilization, waste management, food safety, animal reproduction, health and well being, and emerging technologies and opportunities in animal agriculture.

title: Honors: The Physiology of Reproduction and Lactation
Horse Handling and Care
description: Proper procedures for horse-human interaction and the recommended management procedures for horse care. The basic behavioral characteristics of the horse, an understanding of his physical and mental parameters and their use in horse-human communication. Interactions include imprinting, haltering, halter training, lounging, long-line driving, bridling, bitting, round pen training, saddling, and teaching to guide. Basic care includes feed selection and management, post-natal care, restraint, foot care, dental care, grooming, loading and trailering, stall maintenance, internal and external parasite control, exercising, identification techniques, routine vaccinations and first aid. Safety for both horse and handler will be emphasized.

title: The Physiology of Reproduction and Lactation
description: Biology of sex and sexual differentiation, functional anatomy of male and female, reproduction and lactation, gametogenesis, neuroendocrinology and endocrinology of reproduction and lactation, sex cycles, folliculogenesis, ovulation, spermatogenesis, fertilization, embryonic development, implantation, pregnancy, parturition, initiation of lactation and maintenance of the dry period, artificial control of reproduction and lactation.

title: Seminar in Agricultural Economics and Business
description: Practice of critical thinking, ethical behavior, teamwork, and conflict resolution within the content of agribusiness decision-making. Analysis of contemporary issues in the field of agricultural economics.

title: Animal Health Management
description: Characteristics, symptoms, prevention, and treatment of major diseases and parasites. Immunization, health regulations and herd health programs for all farm livestock species and poultry.

title: Veterinary Entomology
description: (See Entomology and Plant Pathology 325.)

title: Horse, Dairy, and Meat Animal Evaluation
description: Visual and objective appraisal and evaluation of beef cattle, swine, and sheep for functional efficiency. Comparative dairy judging, oral reasons, breed classification programs, economic value of conformation traits. Evaluation of horses for soundness and functional efficiency and the relationship of form to function in various breeds of horses.

title: Animal Nutrition and Production Systems
description: Fundamentals of production and management systems with an emphasis on nutrition in beef, dairy, pork, and poultry programs. Application of principles of nutrition, breeding, physiology, and marketing into enterprise systems. Decision-making management practices and information resources, enterprise evaluation, and comparison of production systems.

title: Advanced Reproduction
description: Collection, evaluation, and preservation of ova, spermatozoa and embryos; application of methods of natural breeding and techniques of artificial insemination and embryo transfer; herd sire and dam evaluation; pregnancy determination; gestation and parturition; infertility; recent advances in theriogenology.

title: Careers Seminar
description: Preparing students for career opportunities in animal agriculture, including both industry and academic advancement. Topics will include resume preparation, interview skills, internship opportunities, and web-based employment search guides.

title: Dairy Cattle Production and Management
description: Integration of principles of nutrition, breeding, physiology, and marketing into complete production and management programs. Structure of industry, enterprise establishment, systems of production, production practices and herd improvement programs. Alternatives evaluated in terms of production responses and economic returns.

title: Pork Production and Management
description: Integration of principles of nutrition, breeding, physiology, and marketing into complete production and management programs. Structure of industry, enterprise establishment, systems of production, production practices, and improvement program. Management evaluated in terms of production responses and economic returns.

title: Poultry Production and Management
description: Integration of principles of nutrition, breeding, physiology, and marketing into complete production and management programs. Structure of industry, enterprise establishment, systems of production, production practices, and improvement programs. Management evaluated in terms of production responses and economic returns.

title: Companion, Zoo, and Lab Animal Management
Ethics in Animal Agriculture
description: Discussion and presentations on issues related to ethics in animal research and industry.

title: Advanced Beef Cattle, Dairy Cattle, Horse, Poultry, Sheep and Swine Judging
Prehistoric Archaeology
description: Introduction to methods and techniques used to identify and date archaeological cultures, reconstruct past lifeways and describe cultural evolution. Overview of the prehistory of Africa, western Europe, southwest Asia, and the Americas from earliest dated human cultures to rise of complex civilizations.

title: Honors: Human Origins
description: Honors survey of humanity's background, fossil primates, fossil human remains, and living races of humankind.

title: Honors: Prehistoric Archaeology
description: Honors introduction to methods and techniques used to identify and date archaeological cultures, reconstruct past lifeways and describe cultural evolution. Overview of the prehistory of Africa, western Europe, southwest Asia, and the Americas from earliest dated human cultures to rise of complex civilizations.

title: Cultural Anthropology
description: Major concepts and methods in the study of culture; survey of cross-cultural similarities and differences in subsistence, social organization, economic, political, and religious institutions; language, ideology and arts. Contributions of anthropology to resolving contemporary human problems.

title: Anthropology of Religion
description: (See Religious Studies 302.)

title: Genetics and Society
description: (See Biochemistry and Cellular and Molecular Biology 306.)

title: Evolution and Society
description: (See Ecology and Evolutionary Biology 305.)

title: North American Indians
description: Comparative overview of Indian cultures of North America. Topical coverage ranges from prehistory and aboriginal lifeways to problems resulting from contact and acculturation. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Southeastern Indians
description: Survey of Southeastern American Indian cultures at the time of European contact. Emphasis on Cherokee culture and on the social, economic, and religious organization of aboriginal groups.

title: Agricultural Finance
description: Macro-finance, financial objectives, acquisition of debt and equity funds, capital investments, capital allocation, debt repayment, credit analysis, borrower and lender loan application analysis, insurance strategies, computer applications, kinds and sources of agricultural credit, and financial intermediation.

title: Honors: Cultural Anthropology
description: Honors introduction to major concepts and methods in the study of culture; survey of cross-cultural similarities and differences in subsistence, social organization, economic, political, and religious institutions; language, ideology and arts. Contributions of anthropology to resolving contemporary human problems.

title: Principles of Biological Anthropology
description: Mechanisms of biological evolution and adaptation in living and prehistoric humans.

title: Peoples and Cultures of Mesoamerica
description: Pre-Columbian and Hispanic cultures of Mexico, Guatemala, Belize, El Salvador and Honduras. Patterns of cultural continuity and cultural change throughout Mesoamerica's history. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Human Origins
description: Survey of humanity's background, fossil primates, fossil human remains, and living races of humankind.

title: Caribbean Cultures and Societies
description: Anthropological approaches to key aspects of Caribbean history, sociocultural pluralism, racial and class stratification, patterns of economic development, and local and national-level political processes. Writing-emphasis course.

title: The African Diaspora
description: An overview of anthropological perspectives on people of African descent and the impact of an African presence on societies in the Americas. The sociocultural experiences of U.S. African-Americans and their counterparts elsewhere in the hemisphere are situated in the context of a broader diaspora. Writing-emphasis course.

title: American Cultures
description: Anthropological perspectives on cultural diversity in America, including the immigrant experience and expressions of ethnicity, intercultural relations, occupational and interest group subcultures. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Topics in African Ethnography
description: Overview of culture patterns and ethnographic research on selected social groups or culture areas in Africa. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Topics in Latin American Ethnography
description: Overview of culture patterns and ethnographic research on selected social groups or culture areas in Latin America. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Migration and Transnationalism
description: Case studies of immigrant, refugee, and displaced populations and changing patterns of culture, identity, and community under conditions of globalization. Writing-emphasis course.

title: North American Prehistory
description: Prehistoric cultures of North America from initial occupation of the continent to European contact. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Junior Honors in Anthropology
description: Analytical, integrative review of current directions of research and theory in anthropology.

title: Historical Archaeology
description: Historical archaeology of Euro-American, African-American, and Asian American cultures in the United States from 15th to 20th centuries.

title: Principles of Archaeology
description: Research strategies used in developing method and theory, constructing cultural histories, identifying site function and settlement-subsistence patterns, and evaluating explanations of cultural change.

title: Prehistory of Tennessee
description: Archaeological principles and theory illustrated in history of archaeological research in Tennessee and through survey of prehistoric Indian cultures from initial occupation of the state to European contact. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Linguistic Anthropology
description: Basic linguistic concepts applied to research in cultural anthropology, particularly investigation of relationships between language and culture.

title: African Religions
description: (See Religious Studies 373.)

title: Environmental Anthropology
description: Overview of theoretical and methodological approaches to the study of human / environmental interactions. Impacts of environmental change on society and culture; human impacts on environmental change.

title: Topics in U.S. Ethnography
description: Overview of culture patterns and ethnographic research on selected social groups or culture areas in the United States. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Principles of Cultural Anthropology
description: Exploration and illustration of major concepts, theories, and methods in cultural anthropology, with application to analysis of specific ethnographies.

title: Peoples and Cultures of South America
description: An introduction to contemporary analysis and debate on South America that places the concept “culture” in historical perspective and discusses the anthropological notion of “people” within the complexity of indigenous and black social formations. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Cities and Sanctuaries of the Greek and Roman World

title: Anatomy and Physiology of Farm Animals
description: Skeleton and joints; muscles; blood and microcirculation; the nervous, endocrine, cardiovascular, respiratory, and digestive systems; demonstrations of physical-chemical phenomena.

title: Selected Topics in Anthropology
description: Theoretical issues in anthropology for undergraduate students. Topics may include practical experience or laboratory study of anthropological materials.

title: Archaeology of the African Diaspora
description: Historical archaeology of African, North American and Latin American sites relating to the transatlantic slave trade and the experiences of enslaved Africans in the New World from the 15th to the 19th centuries. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Archaeological Resource Management
description: Federal legislation and regulations affecting identification, protection, and management of archaeological resources. Professional ethics and responsibilities and relationship of federal and state agencies, public interest groups, and professional archaeologists in conduct of federally sponsored archaeology.

title: Senior Honors in Anthropology
description: Research and writing of the senior honors thesis.

title: Rise of Complex Civilizations
description: Development of complex societies in Old World from origins of agricultural economics to rise of states. Focus on Mesolithic, Neolithic, and Metal Age lifeways in Africa, Europe, and Asia. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Applied Anthropology
description: Introduction to principles, practice and ethics of anthropology applied to practical problems in non-academic settings. Overview of career opportunities in various domains of applied anthropology.

title: Intensive Survey of the Archaeology of Greece
description: Please enable Javascript for full functionality. title: Human Osteology
description: Intensive examination of the human skeleton.

title: Archaeology of Southeastern United States
description: Archaeological research on prehistoric American Indian cultures in Southeastern United States.

title: Primate Evolution
description: Living and fossil primate taxonomy, ecology, and comparative anatomy. Survey of primate fossil record with emphasis on the origin or major primate lineages.

title: Museum Studies II: Exhibition Planning and Installation
Foreign Study
description: Repeatability: May be repeated. Maximum 15 hours.

title: Off-Campus Study
description: Repeatability: May be repeated. Maximum 15 hours.

title: Anthropology of Human Rights
description: Overview of the development and global spread of modern human rights concepts and instruments, with intensive focus on problems of universal rights, cultural relativism, and the anthropological study of specific human rights issues such as terror, torture, and violence against women. Writing emphasis course.

title: Intermediate Modern Standard Arabic II
description: (See Asian Studies 222.)

title: Animal Science Teaching Assistant
description: Assist the primary instructor in laboratory instruction and demonstrations.

title: Elementary Modern Standard Arabic II
description: (See Asian Studies 122.)

title: Readings in Anthropology
description: Problem-oriented directed readings in anthropology.

title: Honors: Introduction to the Built Environment
description: Students will attend 101 classes with supplementary assignments and/or class meetings.

title: Elementary Modern Standard Arabic I
description: (See Asian Studies 121.)

title: Honors: Architecture and the Built Environment
description: Students will attend 111 classes, with supplementary assignments and/or class meetings.

title: Architecture and the Built Environment
description: An introduction to architecture and the built environment for non-architecture majors. Significance of our surroundings, forces that create them. Creative aspects of design. Survey of examples from local to global. Strategies for individual and collective involvement.

title: History and Theory of Architecture I
description: Architecture and ideas of building and community form in major world cultures from the prehistoric era to about 1500 AD.

title: Introduction to Architecture
description: Introduction to architecture as an intellectual discipline. Design as a creative endeavor central to the discipline and its profession.

title: Honors: History and Theory of Architecture I
description: Students will attend 211 classes, with supplementary assignments and/or class meetings.

title: Modern Architecture: Histories And Theories
description: Examines the history and theory of modern architecture focusing on the late 19th and 20th centuries through broad-based examinations of the question of modernity and specific case studies of buildings, projects, landscapes and theories.

title: Honors: History and Theory of Architecture II
description: Students will attend 212 classes, with supplementary assignments and/or class meetings.

title: Early European Prehistory
description: Origins and evolution of human culture in Europe through the beginnings of settled life. Primary focus on Paleolithic/Mesolithic chronology and lifeways. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Principles of Architectural Design
description: Principles of architectural design emphasizing site, function, circulation, structure, technology, context and expression of building.

title: Architectural Structures I
description: Structural properties of building materials under loading and stress. Mechanics of foundations, properties of cross-sections of structural members and analysis of statically determinate trusses, beams, columns, and simple assemblies.

title: History and Theory of Architecture II
description: Architecture and ideas of building and community form in major world cultures from 1500 AD to the mid-20th century.

title: Environmental Control Systems I
description: Principles of active and passive indoor climate control systems, including energy considerations, heating, ventilation, air-conditioning, solar cycles, codes, life-cycle costs, and sustainability, integrated with the building design and envelope. Potable, waste and storm water systems. Life safety related to fire protection systems and codes. Impact of environmental systems on human behavior, comfort, and the environment.

title: Materials and Methods of Construction
description: Properties of interior and exterior building materials and their relation to construction methods and detailing. Theory of material selection and application and the role materials and methods play in the design process.

title: Design Fundamentals II: Space
description: Fundamentals of architectural design and analysis. Diagramming to communicate analysis of design ideas and principles. Design projects with site implications, scale and spatial sequence. Development of architectural representation.

title: History and Theory of Urban Form
Preservation Technology
description: Techniques of preservation. Methods of analysis, history of materials and technology used in old buildings.

title: Representation III: Digital Media
description: An introduction to computer systems, software and hardware, as well as their application in architecture. Emphasis is placed on learning how the computer can assist in the design process by modeling, visualizing and analyzing building designs. Introduction to computer-aided representations, three-dimensional modeling, and desktop publishing.

title: Structures in Architecture II
description: Continuation of analysis and design of simple structures in wood, steel, and concrete. Introduction of building codes, loading tables, and handbooks for selection of structural members.

title: Principles of Environmental Control II
description: Introduction to electrical design and wiring, lighting and acoustics in buildings.

title: Museum Studies II: Exhibition, Planning and Installation
Museum Studies I: Museums, Purpose and Function
Introduction to Studio Art: Various Media
description: Individual sections for various artistic disciplines.

title: Advanced Architectural Design: Development and Design
Environmental Control Systems II
description: Building service systems and lighting design, including principles of electricity, wiring, daylighting, acoustics, and relevant codes, integrated with building envelope materials and assemblies. Principles of sustainability and the impact of environmental systems on human behavior, comfort, and the environment.

title: Ceramics: Portfolio Review
description: Review of prior work in ceramics.

title: The Idea of Graphic Design
description: An overview of design as visual message-making and as an act of cultural interpretation. Contemporary and historic design and its forms are examined, along with an introduction to design and creative concepts, and the role of criticism and theory.

title: Introduction to Preservation
description: History, theory, and legal aspects of architectural preservation and restoration.

title: Graphic Design Seminar
description: Discussion of design and professional issues including politics, economics, and ethics for the graphic designer. Culminates in a student-initiated project.

title: Introduction to Studio Art: Various Media
description: Individual sections for various artistic disciplines.

title: Drawing Portfolio Review
description: Review of prior work in drawing.

title: Honors: Art of Africa, Oceania, and Pre-Columbian America
description: Consent of instructor required. Survey of the traditional arts of the cultures of Black Africa, the Pacific and the Americas. Study grounded in reading, writing and discussion. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Art of Africa, Oceania, and Pre-Columbian America
description: Survey of the traditional arts of the cultures of Black Africa, the Pacific and the Americas (focusing primarily on the period before the European conquest). Sculpture, painting, pottery, textiles, architecture and human adornment will all be examined.

title: Western Art I
description: Major monuments in western art with emphasis on Europe from prehistory through the Middle Ages.

title: Western Art II
description: Major monuments in the history of European and American art from the Renaissance to the present.

title: Honors: Western Art II
description: Consent of Department required. Major monuments in the history of European and American art from the Renaissance to the present. Study grounded in reading, writing, and discussion. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Honors: Western Art I
description: Consent of Department required. Major monuments in western art with emphasis on Europe from prehistory through the Middle Ages. Study grounded in reading, writing, and discussion. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Special Topics in Drawing and Painting
Special Topics in Art History
description: Student- or instructor-initiated course offered at convenience of department.

title: Seminar in Art History II
description: Seminar for majors. Builds on 375 but with an emphasis on research and effective written and oral presentation . Capstone class.

title: History of Photography
description: Survey of the history of photography from the introduction of the daguerreotype and calotype to more recent trends. Emphasis will be placed on aesthetics and the use of photography as a medium for artistic expression.

title: Chinese Art of the 20th and 21st Centuries
description: Survey of Chinese art from the late 19th century through the present. Hong Kong, Taiwanese, and expatriate artists are also considered. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Art of China
description: Survey of the art and architecture of China from the Neolithic period to the 20th century. The major achievements of each period are examined in relation to their religious, political, and social contexts. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Early Christian and Byzantine Art to 1350
description: Art in Italy and the Eastern Empire from the beginnings of Christian art to c. 1350. Mosaic and painting, sculpture and architecture. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Medieval Art of the West, 800-1400
description: Western European art of the Dark Ages, Romanesque, and Gothic periods. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Northern European Painting, 1350-1600
description: From courtly art of late Middle Ages to Northern Renaissance. Jan van Eyck, Roger van der Weyden, and D’rer; early printmakers. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Art of Northern Europe, 1600-1675
description: Concentrated study of Bruegel, Rubens, Rembrandt, Georges de La Tour, Vermeer, Poussin, and Hals. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Art of Southern Europe, 1575-1700
description: Concentrated study of Caravaggio, Bernini, and Italian Baroque developments in all media. Spanish Baroque painting and sculpture with special attention to Velazquez. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Renaissance and Baroque Theory
description: Addresses the theory of Western art in the early modern period with emphasis on the development and evolution in European art during the Renaissance and Baroque periods. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Art of Southern and Eastern Africa
description: Art traditions of the eastern and southern regions of Africa. Sculpture, painting, pottery, textiles, architecture, and human adornment will be examined. Some ancient Stone and Iron Age traditions will be examined, but the main emphasis will be on the diverse ethnic and regional art traditions practiced in the area from the 19th century to the present. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Art and Archaeology of Ancient Africa
description: Historical art traditions of sub-Sahara Africa. Topics to be covered include prehistoric rock paintings, art from archaeological sites and ancient kingdoms. The time period covered ranges from the first and second millennia BC for some of the early terracotta sculpture and rock paintings, the 11th through 19th centuries AD for the later ancient kingdoms. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Arts of the African Diaspora
description: Examines the aesthetic, philosophical and religious patterns of the African descendants of Brazil, Surinam, the Caribbean and the United States. Emphasis will be placed on the full range of art forms, including the sculptural and performance traditions, as well as architecture, textile, basketry, and pottery art forms. Writing-emphasis course.

title: The Art of Italy, 1250-1450
description: Development of exploration of naturalism. Revival of antiquity and development of theories of perspective in the Early Renaissance. Including Duccio, Giotto, Masaccio, Donatello, Botticelli. Writing-emphasis course.

title: African-American Art
description: Traces the artistic and social legacy of African-American art from the eighteenth century to the present day. Specifically, this class will focus on the ways in which artists used creativity to confront, deny, or complicate understandings of racial identity and racism. Examines broad scope of artistic production including painting, sculpture, photography, multi-media, fiction writing, and video art. Writing-emphasis course.

title: History of 20th-Century American Art
description: Developments in architecture, painting, and design from 1900. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Asian Art
description: Selected works of painting, sculpture, architecture, and other forms in India, China, Japan, and to a lesser extent, Korea and Southeast Asia from antiquity through the 19th century.

title: History of 19th-Century Painting and Sculpture in Europe
description: The evolution of Romanticism, Neoclassicism, and Realism in Europe, including the innovations of Manet, Impressionism, Cezanne, Post-Impressionism, Art Nouveau, and Symbolism. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Honors: Asian Art
description: Consent of instructor required. Selected works of painting, sculpture, architecture and other forms in India, China, Japan, Korea and Southeast Asia, from antiquity through the 19th century. Study grounded in reading, writing, and discussion. Writing-emphasis course.

title: History of 20th-Century Painting and Sculpture in Europe
description: Development of the Modern and Post-Modern movements in Europe. Investigation of the progression of abstraction through more recent conceptual trends. Analysis of the work of individual artists such as Picasso, Matisse, and many others. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Studies in Art History
description: Concentration in individually selected area.

title: Art of South and Southeast Asia
description: Survey of the art and architecture of the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia from 2000 BC to the 20th century. The major achievements of each period are examined in relation to their religious, political, and social contexts. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Individual Problems
description: Repeatability: May be repeated. Maximum 12 hours.

title: Independent Study
description: Repeatability: May be repeated. Maximum 9 hours.

title: Introduction to Studio Art: Various Media
description: Individual sections for various artistic disciplines.

title: Introduction to Performance as Art
description: Development of basic concepts and techniques for the creation of performance as an art form.

title: Art of Japan
description: Survey of the art and architecture of Japan from the Neolithic period to the 20th century. The major achievements of each period are examined in relation to their religious, political, and social contexts. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Art of Italy, 1450-1575
description: Concentrated study of Leonardo da Vinci, Michelangelo, Titian, Raphael, Pontormo, and Giorgione. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Introduction to Video Art
description: Development of basic concepts and techniques for the creation of video works as an art form.

title: Special Topics in Media Arts
description: Student- or instructor-initiated course offered at convenience of department.

title: Media Arts Portfolio Review
description: Review of prior work in media arts.

title: Introduction to Sound Art
description: Development of basic concepts and techniques for the creation of sound works as art forms.

title: Oceanic Art
description: Concentrated study of selected sculpture, textiles, architecture and other traditional art forms of Polynesia, Micronesia, and Melanesia. Objects are discussed on the basis of style, style relationship, iconography and the uses to which they were put in their traditional religious, political, and social contexts. Writing-emphasis course.

title: 19th-Century American Art
description: Examines painting, sculpture, and print culture from the Revolutionary War to the turn of the 20th century. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Experiments in Space
description: Advanced study and development of art or design works based on the concepts and techniques of spatiality.

title: Special Topics in Art History
description: Student- or instructor-initiated course offered at convenience of department.

title: Cinematography as Art
description: Continued development of concepts and techniques for the creation of film as an art form with an emphasis on individual projects.

title: Independent Study
description: Repeatability: May be repeated. Maximum 15 hours.

title: Individual Problems
description: Repeatability: May be repeated. Maximum 12 hours.

title: Introduction to Studio Art: Various Media
description: Individual sections for various artistic disciplines.

title: Painting II
description: Techniques of expression in oil and/or acrylic.

title: Watercolor II
description: Capacities of transparent watercolor with attention to individual exploration of surface, space, and concept.

title: Photography III
description: Individual development of photographic problems and techniques.

title: Special Topics in Drawing/Painting
description: Student- or instructor-initiated course offered at convenience of department to enhance and expand the painting, drawing, and watercolor curriculum.

title: Watercolor IV
description: Advanced painting with water-based media on paper stressing individual concepts and personal approaches.

title: Watercolor Portfolio Review
description: Review of prior work in watercolor.

title: Senior Project
description: Students will engage in self-initiated productions to demonstrate proficiency in media art.

title: Capstone
description: Students will engage in self-initiated research to demonstrate proficiency in painting .

title: Intaglio I
description: Metal plate intaglio printing in traditional and contemporary techniques of etching, softground, drypoint, aquatint, and color methods.

title: Lithography I
description: Stone and aluminum plate lithography applying traditional and contemporary techniques of crayon, tusche, transfer methods, state proofs, and photolithography.

title: Sound Art
description: Advanced study and development of concepts and techniques for the creation of sound art with a focus on multidisciplinary forms.

title: Printmaking Portfolio Review
description: Review of prior work in printmaking.

title: Intermediate Print Workshop
description: Individual and collaborative studio work encompassing theory and practice in intaglio, lithography, relief printing, screenprinting, monoprint, papermaking, book arts, and/or photo-print processes.

title: Watercolor I: Introduction
description: Capacities of transparent watercolor.

title: History of Film and Modern Art
description: Study of the development and interaction between the cinematic arts and the visual arts within the context of modern art history.

title: Painting IV
description: Advanced painting stressing individual concepts and personal expression with varied media.

title: Visiting Artist Seminar
description: Study and discussion of contemporary art issues conducted by different visiting artists each semester.

title: Introduction to Studio Art: Various Media
description: Individual sections for various artistic disciplines.

title: Papermaking Workshop
description: Papermaking as a medium for two- and three-dimensional art. Includes sheet forming, imbedding, laminating, embossing, pulp dyeing, inlaying, casting, and other related techniques. Emphasis on development of a personal form.

title: Special Topics in Drawing and Painting
Individual Problems
description: Repeatability: May be repeated. Maximum 12 hours.

title: Intermediate Chinese II
description: (Same as Chinese 232.)

title: Intermediate Japanese II
description: (Same as Japanese 252.)

title: Chinese Literature in English Translation
description: Vernacular and modern literature. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Japanese Literature in English Translation
description: Classical/traditional ― masterpieces of poetry, fiction, and drama to 1868. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Japanese Literature in English Translation
description: Modern ― masterpieces of fiction since 1868. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Visiting Artist Seminar
description: Study and discussion of contemporary art issues conducted by different visiting artists each semester.

title: Asian Film
description: An examination of Asian national cinemas in historical and cultural context. Taught in English. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Chinese Literature in English Translation
description: Classical literature. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Readings in Modern Japanese Literature
description: (Same as Japanese 452.)

title: Chinese and Japanese Foreign Study
description: Repeatability: May be repeated. Maximum 15 hours.

title: Japanese Graphic Novels and Animation
description: Reading and analysis of major contemporary Japanese graphic novels with special attention to related works of film and television animation. All readings are in English translation. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Intermediate Modern Standard Arabic I
description: Taped language program.

title: Topics in Japanese Literature
description: In English with readings in Japanese for minors. Writing-emphasis course.

title: Intermediate Modern Hebrew II
description: Taped language program.

description: Taped language program.
